name: Foundry Local On-Device AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Cloud escalation endpoint — handles complex queries that exceed on-device model capabilities, multi-step reasoning, large context windows, and high-accuracy requirements

dev.note: ~30K tokens/day, escalation testing and model comparison

prod.note: ~200K tokens/day, ~20% of queries escalated to cloud

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for guaranteed cloud escalation SLA

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Device fleet management — model deployment to edge devices, telemetry collection, device health monitoring, OTA model updates, escalation routing

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Fleet observability — device health metrics, on-device inference latency, escalation rates, model accuracy tracking, battery/resource impact

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Model registry and distribution — on-device model packages (ONNX, GGUF), update payloads, telemetry archives, escalation logs

services.name: Azure Container Registry
services.purpose: Edge runtime images — Foundry Local runtime containers, model serving layers, device-specific optimizations (ARM64, x86, GPU variants)

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Device provisioning secrets — IoT Hub connection strings, model signing certificates, cloud escalation API keys, device attestation keys

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Cloud-side orchestration — escalation routing logic, model update scheduling, telemetry aggregation, anomaly detection on fleet health

optimization_tips: Keep 80-90% of inference on-device using Phi-4-mini or similar SLMs — only escalate to cloud GPT-4o for queries exceeding local model confidence thresholds, keeping cloud costs proportional to complexity
Use delta model updates via IoT Hub instead of full model re-downloads — diff-based ONNX updates reduce bandwidth costs by 85% and minimize device downtime
Batch telemetry uploads to IoT Hub — aggregate device metrics locally every 5 minutes instead of per-inference streaming, reducing message counts by 90%
Implement on-device response caching with semantic similarity matching — cache frequent query responses locally to avoid repeated inference for similar prompts
Use Azure Monitor basic metrics (free tier) for device health and reserve pay-per-GB for detailed inference logs — most fleet monitoring needs are met with standard IoT Hub metrics
Schedule model deployments during off-peak network hours — stagger updates across device fleet to avoid bandwidth spikes and reduce CDN/egress costs
